children.blurb: “He bolts the die.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about using things thoughtlessly.
“For war men crave.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about the venerable Ananda.
“Trust not the trusted.” This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about taking things on trust.
“Now scorched.”—This story the Master told while at Patikarama near Vesali, about Sunakkhatta.
“How transient.”—This story was told by the Master as he lay on his death-bed, concerning Ananda’s words, “O Blessed One, suffer not your end to be in this sorry little town.”
“As one with care.”—This story was told by the Master while dwelling in a forest near the town of Desaka in the Sumbha country, concerning the Janapada-Kalyani Sutta.

children.translated_name: One Who Thought Names Had Power
children.blurb: “Seeing Quick dead.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a Brother who thought luck went by names.

“Wise rightly, Wisest wrongly.”—This story was told by the Master while at Jetavana, about a cheating merchant. There were two merchants in partnership at Savatthi, we are told, who travelled with their merchandise and came back with the proceeds.
“Far better than a thousand fools.”—This story was told by the Master when at Jetavana, concerning the question of the unconverted.

“In guise of joy.”—This story was told by the Master while at Kundadhanavana near the city of Kundiya about Suppavasa, a lay-sister, who was daughter to King Koliya. For at that time, she, who had carried a child seven years in her womb, was in the seventh day of her throes, and her pains were grievous.
